title: eragrostis pilosa var. tef
intro: Eragrostis pilosa var. tef, also known as tef, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae and is native to the highlands of Ethiopia. Tef has been traditionally used in Ethiopian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antifungal, and antibacterial activities. The health benefits of tef include its ability to improve digestion, lower blood pressure, and reduce the risk of certain diseases such as diabetes and cardiovascular disease. Tef can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory problems and skin infections.
